title: Python workspace generated-runtime relocation

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Advance #892 by moving generated workspace outputs off direct imports from agentic_workspace.workspace_runtime_primitives and onto generated-local runtime bindings.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not delete source runtime files until generated outputs, package entrypoints, tests, and packaging prove replacement ownership; generated outputs must be reproducible from command-generation; installed console behavior must remain green.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Generated-local module names, import seams, and whether this slice relocates operation-executor handlers, command adapter handlers, or both.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A safe fix requires copying the entire workspace runtime module as opaque generated code, changing public command behavior, or broadening into memory/planning package runtime relocation.
canonical_core.next_action: Render generated-local workspace runtime binding modules and update generated workspace command and operation executor imports to use those generated-local modules.
proof_expectations: uv run ruff check affected generator/check/generated files
completion_criteria: Generated workspace command modules and operation executor no longer import agentic_workspace.workspace_runtime_primitives directly.
The new generated-local runtime binding is produced by command-generation and packaged with generated/workspace/python.
Static proof is updated to recognize the generated-local workspace runtime seam without claiming full #892 completion.
Python conformance, Docker conformance, workspace CLI tests, and generated freshness pass.

non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

execution_run.what happened: Rendered contract-driven generated-local workspace runtime binding modules and updated generated workspace command modules plus operation executor handlers to import through those generated-local facades instead of importing agentic_workspace.workspace_runtime_primitives directly.
execution_run.scope touched: workspace generated Python command projection, command-generation renderer, command package IR/schema, static proof, and proof tests
execution_run.validations run: uv run python scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py --check; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-conformance; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-docker-conformance --require-docker; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli_blackbox.py tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py -q; uv run ruff check affected generator/check/generated/test files
execution_run.result for continuation: direct source-runtime imports are removed from generated workspace commands and operation executor, but generated/workspace/python/primitives/workspace_runtime.py still re-exports source runtime and remains a #892 blocker
execution_run.next step: Replace generated workspace facade source-runtime re-exports with generated/codegen-owned primitive implementations or an explicitly generated product runtime projection, then continue memory and planning runtime blockers.

finished_run_review.intent served: partially: generated workspace direct imports were relocated, but #892 remains open
finished_run_review.config compliance: used uv run agentic-workspace and contract/generation paths; did not use bare CLI
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: medium if this slice is mistaken for full #892 completion; static proof still rejects that
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: continue #892 on the next runtime blocker

delegation_outcome_feedback.route skipped reason: Workspace runtime relocation is high-blast-radius and must preserve generated console behavior; delegate only after the import/binding seam is proven.
delegation_outcome_feedback.actual friction: workspace test caught an unacceptable package-specific import literal in command-generation; fixed by moving local runtime binding metadata into command_package_ir instead of hard-coding workspace names in the generator
delegation_outcome_feedback.proof result: passed after correcting the package-specific generator violation
delegation_outcome_feedback.quality concern: Avoid moving source code without generated ownership proof; generated outputs must be reproducible and installed entrypoints must still work.
delegation_outcome_feedback.decomposition adjustment: Start with workspace runtime import/binding relocation before memory and planning package runtime blockers.

proof_report.proof achieved now: Generated workspace command modules and operation executor no longer import agentic_workspace.workspace_runtime_primitives directly; generated-local runtime facade is rendered from command_package_ir local_runtime_bindings; static proof still rejects full completion while facade source-runtime re-exports remain.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: uv run python scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py --check; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-conformance; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-docker-conformance --require-docker passed after one unchanged rerun; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli_blackbox.py tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py -q. #1002 tracks the failed-run-then-green Docker proof behavior.

intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: The direct-import relocation sub-slice is satisfied, but the generated facade still re-exports source runtime, so the workspace source runtime blocker is not fully eliminated.
intent_satisfaction.unsolved intent passed to: continue #892: replace generated workspace facade source-runtime re-exports, then continue memory and planning blockers

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Generated workspace commands and operation executor now route source-runtime references through a generated-local facade described by contract-owned local_runtime_bindings instead of generator hard-coding or direct imports.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: #1002 filed for Docker conformance failed-run-then-green proof behavior
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): command_package_ir schema now owns local_runtime_bindings metadata
execution_summary.resume from: next #892 runtime blocker: workspace facade source-runtime elimination

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.
closure_check.why this decision is honest: The bounded direct-import relocation slice is complete, but #892 is not complete because generated workspace facade source-runtime re-exports and memory/planning runtime blockers remain.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Static proof, Python conformance, Docker conformance, workspace tests, and ruff passed; #1002 records transient Docker proof instability observed during closeout.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Generated workspace commands or operation executor again import source runtime directly, or full #892 completion is claimed while facade source-runtime re-exports remain.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.

continuation: Continue #892 with workspace facade source-runtime elimination, then memory runtime relocation, then planning runtime relocation.
issue_follow_up: #1002 Dogfooding: Docker conformance can fail once and pass unchanged on rerun
